hypothesis: The score-difference vector rSAA(x,t) = sBθ(x,t) − sAθ(x,t) computed from a diffusion model trained exclusively on metastable-state configurations constitutes a globally valid one-dimensional reaction coordinate such that using its projection as the sole collective variable in enhanced-sampling simulations (e.g., metadynamics) reproduces the committor distribution (mean and variance) and transition rate within 20% of values obtained from long unbiased MD while requiring at least an order-of-magnitude less simulation effort.
